ops.title: In this machine's jj-colocated repos, hook-triggered jj reconciliation can delete uncommitted files between Bash calls — restore+commit in one call
ops.body:
During the cc-sudo bootstrap, files that were written but not yet committed (README.md, CHANGELOG.md, docs assets) were deleted from the working tree between Bash calls, and two `git add … && git commit` chains produced empty commits. The colocated jj repo reconciles against git HEAD moves when post-tool hooks (capt-hook/cc-notes) run jj, and sweeps files it considers abandoned.

**Why:** hooks run between Bash calls, so any state that exists only in the working tree or index is at risk in a jj-colocated repo.

**How to apply:** in jj-colocated repos, do restore/add/commit in a single Bash invocation, verify each commit landed non-empty (`git show --stat`), and expect git HEAD to detach after jj activity — re-attach with `git checkout -B main <sha>` before pushing. Orphaned commits keep the content recoverable (`git checkout <sha> -- <paths>`).
